2605.25864.title: When Self-Belief Misleads: Active Label Acquisition for Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards
2605.25864.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable advancements in reasoning capabilities empowered by Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR). Nonetheless, RLVR intrinsically relies on ground-truth labels for reward computation, the acquisition of which is often prohibitively expensive in real-world scenarios. While unsupervised RLVR paradigms attempt to circumvent this by training on pseudo-labels, they are notoriously susceptible to training collapse. Moreover, different samples often exhibit varying annotation values. In this paper, we propose Reinforcement Learning with Active Verifiable Rewards (RLAVR), which actively acquires ground-truth labels for a small set of selected samples and integrates them with pseudo-labels, thereby stabilizing training dynamics and improving performance under limited annotation budgets. To identify valuable samples, we propose the Corrective Advantage Gap (CAG) metric and analyze the sample-level supervision value. Building on this, we introduce Correction-Aware Reliability Estimation for RLAVR (CARE), which translates the oracle CAG criterion into a practical pre-query acquisition policy to substantially improve training stability. Extensive experiments across diverse domains, model families, and model scales demonstrate the effectiveness and generality of our approach. Our code is available at https://github.com/Lumina04/CARE.
2605.25864.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在推理能力上取得了顯著的進展，這得益於可驗證獎勵的強化學習（RLVR）。然而，RLVR 本質上依賴於真實標籤來計算獎勵，而在現實場景中獲取這些標籤往往代價高昂。雖然無監督的 RLVR 範式試圖通過在偽標籤上進行訓練來迴避這一問題，但它們在訓練過程中極易崩潰。此外，不同樣本往往表現出不同的標註值。在本文中，我們提出了主動可驗證獎勵的強化學習（RLAVR），該方法主動獲取一小組選定樣本的真實標籤，並將其與偽標籤整合，從而穩定訓練動態並在有限的標註預算下提高性能。為了識別有價值的樣本，我們提出了修正優勢差距（CAG）度量並分析樣本級別的監督價值。在此基礎上，我們引入了 RLAVR 的修正感知可靠性估計（CARE），該方法將 oracle CAG 標準轉化為實用的預查詢獲取策略，以顯著提高訓練穩定性。在多個領域、模型系列和模型規模上進行的廣泛實驗證明了我們方法的有效性和普遍性。我們的代碼可在 https://github.com/Lumina04/CARE 獲得。
2605.25864.author: Li Wang et.al.
2605.25864.authors: Li Wang, Xiaodong Lu, Xiaohan Wang, Yikun Ban, Jiajun Chai, Wei Lin, Tianhao Peng, Guojun Yin
